With respect to employees who engage in harmful or dangerous work as defined by KOSHA, the employer shall not require the employee to work in excess of six (6) hours per day or thirty four (34) hours per week for high risk work. \nInclude hours worked by salaried, hourly, part-time and workers including temporary help services workers under your supervision, even if they were not under your direct employment.\nDo not include vacation, sick leave, holidays, or any other non-work time, even if employees were paid for it. If your establishment keeps records of only the hours paid or if you have employees who are not paid by the hour, please estimate the hours that the employees actually worked. If this number isn't available, an estimate is still needed.
Please enter the total number of hours worked by all employees during the given year. Please round your answer to the next highest whole number.

Industrial accidents, injuries, or illnesses/diseases that result in an employee death. A serious accident is defined as follows: 1) An accident resulting in one or more fatalities. 2) An accident where two or more people are simultaneously injured, requiring medical treatment for three months or longer. 3) An accident where ten or more people are simultaneously injured or suffer from occupational diseases. Article 2, Clause 2
Please enter the number of fatalities for the given year.
Lost time industrial accidents are defined as those incidents where the employee missed more than a day of work due to a work related injury.
Please enter the number of industrial accidents for the given year which resulted in employee lost time, excluding fatalities.

### High Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *HIGHER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

### Low Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *LOWER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time and part-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

The term "industrial accident" means any death, injury, or disease of a person who provides labor caused by structures, equipment, raw materials, gas, vapor, powder, dust, etc., related to the duties, or by work or other duties.\nArticle 2, Clause 1
Please enter the number of industrial accidents, excluding fatalities, that are classified as over-three-day injuries for the given year.

The term "industrial accident" means any death, injury, or disease of a person who provides labor caused by structures, equipment, raw materials, gas, vapor, powder, dust, etc., related to the duties, or by work or other duties. Article 2, Clause 1
Please enter the number of industrial accidents, excluding fatalities, that are classified as under-three-day injuries for the given year.

Medical treatment (no lost time) refers to work-related injuries where the employee received treatment from a physician or medical professional beyond simple first aid (such as stitches, prescription medication, or other clinical procedures), but did not miss any shifts or workdays (other than the day of injury). Article 10-2
Please enter the number of workplace injuries that required medical treatment beyond first aid, but did not result in any lost workdays, for the given year.
Please enter the number of first aid cases for the given year.
Please enter the number of reportable dangerous occurrences (or near misses) for the given year.
Please enter the number of lost working days as a result of workplace accidents and occupational diseases for the given year.

The term "reportable disease" means an infectious disease of Class 1 to Class 3 as defined in Article 2 of the Act on the Prevention and Control of Infectious Diseases, which is considered to have a high level of public health importance.\n\nArticle 2 of the Act on the Prevention and Control of Infectious Diseases, Clause 2 to 4 define Class 1 to Class 3 infectious disease
Please enter the number of reportable diseases for the given year.
